comment.text: This text labels a section explaining what the user is paying for, specifically related to AI services. It appears in both the "Get Started" and "Pricing & Billing" sections of the AI service settings, likely as a heading for a list of features or services included in the payment. Translators should ensure the meaning accurately conveys what the user is receiving for their payment.

usage.context: {t("AIWhatYouPayForTitle")}

<ModalDialog.Body>
 <div className={styles.pricingBody}>
 <Text fontSize="12px">{t("AIPricingIntro")}</Text>
 
 <Text fontSize="16px" fontWeight="700">
 {t("AIWhatYouPayForTitle")}
 </Text>
 
 <div className={styles.pricingSection}>
 <div className={styles.pricingSectionHeader}>
 <Text fontSize="14px" fontWeight="700">

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Bulgarian translation 'Какво плащате за' is a literal word-for-word translation that is grammatically incorrect and sounds unnatural in Bulgarian. In Bulgarian, you cannot end a sentence/heading with the preposition 'за' in this context. It should be phrased as 'Какво включва плащането ви' (What your payment includes) or 'За какво плащате' (What you are paying for).

The Spanish translation 'Lo que paga por' is a literal, word-for-word translation that is grammatically incorrect and sounds unnatural in Spanish. In Spanish, you cannot end a sentence with a preposition like 'por' in this context. It sounds like an incomplete thought.

The French translation 'Ce que vous payez pour' is a literal word-for-word translation of the English prepositional phrase, which is grammatically incorrect and sounds unnatural in French. In French, a sentence cannot end with 'pour'.

The Lao translation 'ສິ່ງທີ່ທ່ານຈ່າຍຄ່າ' is grammatically incomplete and translates more like 'The thing you pay cost', which sounds unnatural for a heading. It fails to convey the concept of 'what is included in your payment' or 'what you are paying for' in a way that functions as a section title.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: ສິ່ງທີ່ທ່ານຈະໄດ້ຮັບຈາກການຊໍາລະ

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The translation 'O que você paga por' is a literal, word-for-word translation from English that is grammatically incorrect and unnatural in Portuguese. In Portuguese, you cannot end a sentence/heading with the preposition 'por' in this context.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: O que está incluso no seu pagamento

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The translation 'O que você paga por' is a literal word-for-word translation that results in an ungrammatical and unnatural sentence in Portuguese. In this context (a heading for a list of features), it should convey 'What is included in your payment' or 'What you are paying for'.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: O que está incluso no seu plano

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The translation contains a typo/nonsense word 'Штато' which is not a valid Serbian word. It appears to be a concatenation error or a typo for 'Шта' (What).

The Turkish translation 'Neye ödeme yapıyorsunuz' is a question ('What are you paying for?') which implies an inquiry to the user. The English 'What you pay for' is a noun phrase/heading describing a list of included features. The translation should be a descriptive heading, not a question.
